text: Add a top-level Closeout Definition section: 'A closeout means ZERO open/dangling items. Never end a closeout with caveats. Ensure every owner records remaining work, verify idempotent fixed point, commit loose work, then archive.'
Add Definition of Done section: 'When asked to define done or a goal, deliver an EXECUTABLE predicate (script/test), not hand-maintained prose. Done-criteria must be self-verifying and runnable.'

Add Never Over-Claim Completion section: 'Do not declare work done until verified: run all tests/CI gates locally, confirm loops/drivers actually run, check no regressions from merges. Reconcile branches against authoritative GitHub data, not heuristics.'
Add Edits Policy section: 'Prefer minimal in-place edits during closeouts; do not create new files unless asked. Be mindful of case-insensitive filesystems to prevent silent overwrites.'

Create a custom Skill (reusable /closeout command) that captures your verify->close-gaps->archive->handoff pattern, eliminating the need to re-type 'close gaps and closeout cleanly' five times per session.
Use Hooks (PostToolUse Edit|Write matchers) to auto-run CI gate checks (npm run lint) before commits, catching lint/type/accessibility failures locally before push.
Explicitly request Task Agents for parallel codebase exploration, launching focused subagents per domain to avoid token-limit blowups in long sessions.
Fix permission prompts root-to-leaf by adding broad rule patterns instead of single exact commands (e.g., all Python invocations in repo) to prevent 10+ repeated re-prompts.
Avoid token-limit blowups by requesting concise summaries from agents, avoiding full file dumps, and breaking giant closeouts into staged sub-tasks with checkpoints.
Make closeouts demonstrably terminal by proving an idempotent fixed point (re-run verification shows zero changes), then archive—never list open items as caveats.
Executable Done-Predicates as North Star: Write a machine-checkable 'definition of done' predicate at session start that Claude runs to gate its own completion claims.

Parallel Multi-Source Recon Swarm: Dispatch N independent Agent workers per search domain, each returning a structured JSON packet {found, not_found, confidence}, then auto-merge into single ground-truth report.

Self-Healing Green-CI Iteration Loop: In an isolated worktree, run full CI gate matrix locally (lint, types, tests, build, accessibility, deploy), fixing root-to-leaf and re-running until all pass end-to-end.

A closeout means ZERO dangling items. Never end a /closeout with 'but here's what's still open' caveats. If gaps exist, close them first, then confirm the closeout is a terminal, idempotent fixed point verified by green predicate runs.
Never declare work 'done' or 'fully achieved' until verified against ground truth (live git state, passing tests, running loop). Do not over-claim completion.
When defining 'done' or completion criteria, express it as an executable predicate (a runnable check), not hand-maintained prose.
For Python and recurring shell commands, apply broad root-to-leaf allow rules rather than allowlisting single exact commands, to avoid repeated permission prompts.
When a bridge/browser/connectivity check reports blocked, attempt the documented bootstrap that forces reconnection instead of passively re-reporting the blocked status.

Custom Skills: Reusable markdown-defined prompts you invoke with a single /command. Formalizing /closeout as a skill with executable predicate gates baked in would prevent recurring caveat/premature-done friction.
Hooks: Shell commands that auto-run at lifecycle events. A pre-commit hook running lint/type/a11y checks would catch these locally before push.
Task Agents: Focused subagents for parallel exploration and complex system understanding. Leaning on Agent explicitly for exploration will speed ground-truth gathering.
Establish ground truth before claiming done: Make read-only ground-truth checks the mandatory first step of any completion or closeout, then require green predicates before reporting success.
Resolve blockers actively, not passively: Convert passive 'status: blocked' reports into an attempted bootstrap or fix. When a documented recovery path exists, attempt it before reporting.
Fix permissions at the root: When a permission prompt recurs, broaden the allow rule instead of patching one command.
Predicate-Driven Autonomous Closeout Loops: Define completion as an executable predicate that the agent runs until it reaches a green, idempotent fixed point.
Parallel Multi-Agent Ground-Truth Sweeps: For root-cause hunts, launch parallel sub-agents that each independently establish ground truth from live sources (git, GitHub, filesystem, logs).

Isolated Worktree Test-Iteration Pipelines: Build and prove risky changes inside an isolated git worktree so the live fleet is never disrupted, then iterate against tests until every gate is green before merging.
When a task is complete and predicates are green, state 'CLOSEOUT COMPLETE — idempotent fixed point reached, zero dangling items' and STOP. Do not re-recite residual/caveat items as open work.
Define 'done' only as an executable predicate that exits green. Before declaring anything done, re-verify against live ground truth (git/GitHub/deploy state), not a cached or prior status.

At human-gated authorization gates (merge approval, installing launchd agents, PII visibility flips), do NOT stall or loop — clearly state 'BLOCKED: human authorization required for X' once, then continue driving reversible work.
Never guess or ask about decisions that a registry/source-of-truth already owns. Query the registry directly instead of using AskUserQuestion.
All work must happen in the isolated worktree, never the main checkout. Verify the current worktree/branch before committing.
Custom Skills: You invoke /closeout constantly (7+ goals) but Claude keeps stalling — a hardened SKILL.md would encode the 'verify live, run predicate, stop when green' loop deterministically.
Hooks: Claude committed to wrong checkout and hit lint/type/CI blockers repeatedly; a PreToolUse hook on commit can assert worktree isolation and run lint before every commit.
MCP Servers: You hit repeated 'Not logged in', Chrome-bridge-blocked, and PR/CI friction — a GitHub MCP server gives reliable live PR/deploy status without shelling out and stalling.
Break the closeout stall loop: Explicitly tell Claude to stop looping and treat repeated resume prompts as a signal the task is genuinely done.
Force browser/auth bootstrap instead of passive status checks: Tell Claude to actively bootstrap blocked bridges rather than passively reporting them blocked.
Parallelize recon with Task Agents up front: Explicitly request parallel exploration agents at session start for discovery/verification.
Predicate-Driven Autonomous Closeout Loops: Imagine a closeout that only terminates when a green executable predicate proves zero dangling items.
Parallel Recon Agents For Ground-Truth Sweeps: Spawn multiple recon agents that fan out across every remote, local checkout, and log source simultaneously.
Self-Healing CI-To-Merge Pipelines: Point Claude at a red build to autonomously diagnose root cause, fix each, and verify all CI gates pass locally before pushing.
green→'closeout complete, idempotent fixed point'+STOP no re-open. Never AskUserQuestion for charter/registry/constitution-answered decisions.
Private/personal data in encrypted git-tracked location never local-only; correct isolated worktree always, verify path.
Blocked browser→documented bootstrap first; closeout recipe both gate predicates, no residual recitation.
Ruff format+check --fix hook on Edit/Write; task agents for fleet audits; drive all reversible work before presenting human gates.
Measurement-first live ground truth; self-terminating predicate-driven closeout subagent.
Tightened /closeout SKILL.md verify-green-then-terminate; pre-commit ruff hook; parallel recon agents for fleet audits.
Closeout stall loop break; human-gated decision handling reversible-first; ground truth before acting.
Self-terminating autonomous closeout loops; human-gate batching w/ single approval manifest; parallel self-healing PR fleet manager w/ launchd.
Derive via ideal-forms, AskUserQuestion only for genuinely irreducible destructive/irreversible; never wait/poll non-required CI, merge on required only.
All durable state in git-tracked/chezmoi source never local-only; no 'no actionable path' stall, proceed+flag true blockers.
Ship recipe scoped tests→PR→required checks→merge→closeout predicates; ruff hook on Edit/Write w/ $CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS; nine-builder fleet pattern for cross-repo.
/ship skill codifying ship-and-closeout; kill CI-wait by required-checks-only+background monitor; durable-homing as explicit closeout predicate.
Overnight PR pipeline w/ launchd sentinel+morning digest; parallel adversarial-review swarm per-lens unanimous PASS; test-driven self-healing effectors.
More skills beyond /closeout (/ship); pre-commit ruff hook; orchestrated cross-repo agent fleets.
Codify ship-and-closeout loop as skill; kill CI-wait friction; enforce durable homing before done.
Fleet-orchestrated overnight PR pipelines; parallel adversarial review swarm; test-driven self-healing effectors.
Derive from first principles+charter before asking, ask only for missing external info; never block deliverable on slow/non-required CI.
Config/statusline/personal-data to durable git-tracked/chezmoi source; triage/review-window scope from last HUMAN review point not last automated run, sanity-check counts.
Codify closeout+PR-heal-and-merge+adversarial-audit as skills; ruff auto-format hook silent on Edit/Write; parallel agents per repo/branch for cross-repo tranches.
Closeout treats only required checks as gating; derive via ideal-forms before AskUserQuestion; triage anchor last-human-review+count plausibility.
Scoped path-aware gate system mapping diffs to minimal test sets+async CI polling; charter/ideal-forms as derivation layer+pre-action self-check subagent.
Launchd daemons+sanctioned-waiter+idempotency guards+sentinel auto-resume.
Skills for closeout/PR-heal-and-merge/adversarial-audit; pre-commit ruff hook; parallel agents per repo/branch.
Stop polling slow CI; derive don't ask; confirm scope before triage.
Fast-path CI gates; self-deriving agents that stop asking; overnight self-healing agent fleet.
